description: coder-workflow hooks — safety guards, graph maintenance, task tracking, session intelligence. Optimized: deduplicated, lean session-context, added missing hooks.

hooks.command: bash

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); PROMPT=$(jq -r ".prompt // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 140); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] PROMPT: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$PROMPT" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; exit 0'
bash
bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/scripts/gitignore-guard.sh

bash -c 'DB=.codegraph/graph.json; if [ ! -f "$DB" ]; then touch .codegraph/scan.lock 2>/dev/null || true; (command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow scan 2>/dev/null || true); rm -f .codegraph/scan.lock 2>/dev/null || true; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'GLOBAL="$HOME/.claude/skills/coder-workflow/skills"; LOCAL=".claude/skills/coder-workflow"; if [ -d "$GLOBAL" ] && [ -d "$LOCAL" ]; then echo "coder-workflow: Dual install detected. Project-local (.claude/) takes precedence."; echo "  Remove one:"; echo "    rm -rf ~/.claude/skills/coder-workflow"; echo "    rm -rf .claude/skills/coder-workflow"; fi; exit 0'
bash
echo 'Context was compacted then resumed. Skills, orchestrator, CodeGraph MCP tools still active. Re-read active task before proceeding.'
echo 'Session cleared. Use /coder-workflow:coder-orchestrator for any coding task.'
echo 'Nested session start — subagent session initialized.'
bash -c 'mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null; echo 0 > .claude/stop-block.count; exit 0'
bash
bash

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+reset\s+--hard|git\s+clean\s+-[a-zA-Z]*f"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow: git reset --hard or clean -f destroys uncommitted work. Ensure nothing unsaved exists.\"}"; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "DROP\s+(TABLE|DATABASE|SCHEMA)|TRUNCATE\s+TABLE"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow: Destructive SQL detected (DROP/TRUNCATE). Confirm dev/test, not production.\"}"; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+merge"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow: git merge detected. Check conflicts with: git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U\"}"; fi; exit 0'
bash -c 'jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow quality: Before writing — check _shared/OVERPOWERED.md. No suppression flags, no dummy code, no speculation.\"}"'
bash

bash -c 'jq -n "{systemMessage: \"MANDATORY: Use ONLY the explore-codebase custom agent (coder-workflow:explore-codebase) for codebase exploration, powered by CodeGraph MCP tools (query_graph, search_code, analyze_impact). Do NOT use the built-in Explore agent. Use batch multi-pattern search_code with patterns:[...] for efficiency. Do NOT use bash find/grep.\"}"'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); LOG=.claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log; mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; [ -f "$LOG" ] && SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) && [ "$SIZE" -gt 10485760 ] && tail -n +500 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"; printf "[%s] WRITE: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$FILE" >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null; exit 0'

bash -c 'DEBOUNCE_FILE=.codegraph/.update-debounce; LOCK_FILE=.codegraph/.update-debounce.lock; (flock -x 200; NOW=$(date +%s); LAST=0; [ -f "$DEBOUNCE_FILE" ] && LAST=$(cat "$DEBOUNCE_FILE" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^[0-9]+$" || echo 0); ELAPSED=$((NOW - LAST)); if [ "$ELAPSED" -ge 5 ]; then mkdir -p .codegraph 2>/dev/null || true; printf "%s" "$NOW" > "$DEBOUNCE_FILE"; test -f .codegraph/graph.json && command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow update >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi) 200>"$LOCK_FILE"; exit 0'

bash -c 'if command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1; then FILE=$(jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$FILE" ] && [ -f "$FILE" ]; then coder-workflow bughunt scan-file --file "$FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 & fi; fi; exit 0'
bash -c 'test -f .codegraph/graph.json && command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow update >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; exit 0'
hooks.if: Bash(git checkout *)|Bash(git switch *)|Bash(git merge *)|Bash(git pull)|Bash(git rebase *)

hooks.command: bash -c 'printf "[%s] GIT COMMIT: committed\n" "$(date -u +%T)" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] GIT OUTPUT: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$CMD" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0'
bash -c 'mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] INSTALL: dep install detected\n" "$(date -u +%T)" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0'

bash -c 'TOOL=${1:-unknown}; mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] GRAPH: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$TOOL" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0' session-cw-graph-log

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); TOOL=$(jq -r ".tool_name // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); ERR=$(jq -r ".tool_error // .error // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 200); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] FAIL [%s]: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$TOOL" "$ERR" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; if [ -n "$TOOL" ] && [ -n "$ERR" ]; then command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow learn-log --type tool_failure --tool "$TOOL" --error "$ERR" 2>/dev/null || true; fi; exit 0'
echo '=== coder-workflow turn complete ==='
echo 'Rate limit hit. Implement exponential backoff or reduce request rate. Resume with shorter prompts.'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); ERR=$(jq -r ".reason // .error // \"rate_limit\"" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 200); command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow learn-log --type stop_failure --tool rate_limit --error "$ERR" 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0'
echo 'Token limit reached. Shorten response scope or increase max_tokens. Continue with targeted follow-up.'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); ERR=$(jq -r ".reason // .error // \"max_output_tokens\"" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 200); command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow learn-log --type stop_failure --tool max_output_tokens --error "$ERR" 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0'
echo 'API server error. New session or subagent to resume when service recovers.'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); ERR=$(jq -r ".reason // .error // \"server_error\"" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 200); command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow learn-log --type stop_failure --tool server --error "$ERR" 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0'
echo 'Package manifest changed. Run install before using new packages.'
echo 'Env file changed. Verify gitignored and no committed secrets before next git op.'
echo 'CLAUDE.md changed — project instructions updated. New rules apply immediately.'
echo 'hooks.json changed — hook config updated. Takes effect on next hook event.'
echo 'Config changed. Rebuild may be needed: npm run build && npm run typecheck'
echo 'MCP config changed. Restart or reload plugins for new server config.'
bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/scripts/gitignore-guard.sh
echo 'Agent/skill definition changed. New instructions apply to subsequent spawns.'

bash -c 'LOCK=.claude/agent-depth.lock; mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null; printf "[%s] AGENT: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "${1:-unknown}" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; (flock -x 200; DEPTH=$(cat "$LOCK" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^[0-9]+$" || echo 0); NEW=$((DEPTH+1)); printf "%s" "$NEW" > "$LOCK"; if [ "$NEW" -gt 2 ]; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"DEPTH LIMIT: Agent nesting >2 max. Stop delegating. Execute directly.\"}"; fi) 200>.claude/agent-depth.lock.flock; exit 0' subagent-start

bash -c 'LOCK=.claude/agent-depth.lock; (flock -x 200; DEPTH=$(cat "$LOCK" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^[0-9]+$" || echo 1); NEW=$((DEPTH-1)); if [ $NEW -le 0 ]; then rm -f "$LOCK"; else printf "%s" "$NEW" > "$LOCK"; fi) 200>.claude/agent-depth.lock.flock; exit 0'
echo 'Context was compacted. Skills, orchestrator, and CodeGraph tools are still active. Re-read task state before continuing.'

bash -c 'DIR=$(pwd); printf "[%s] CWD: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$DIR" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null || true; test -f .codegraph/graph.json && echo "CodeGraph available: $(stat -c%s .codegraph/graph.json 2>/dev/null || echo 0) bytes" || echo "No CodeGraph graph file in this directory."; exit 0'
bash

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); TITLE=$(jq -r ".task.title // .title // \"unknown\"" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] TASK done: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$TITLE" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; echo "Task complete. Verify: over-engineered? No dummy code? See _shared/OVERPOWERED.md."; exit 0'
bash -c 'if command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1; then coder-workflow dream >/dev/null 2>&1; coder-workflow curate prune >/dev/null 2>&1; fi; exit 0'
bash -c 'mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] PERM DENIED: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "${1:-unknown}" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0' perm-denied-logger
bash -c 'CHANGE=$(cat 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown"); printf "[%s] CONFIG: source=%s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$CHANGE" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0'
bash
bash -c 'if command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1; then coder-workflow dream >/dev/null 2>&1; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'rm -f .claude/agent-depth.lock .claude/agent-depth.lock.flock .claude/stop-block.count 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] SESSION END\n" "$(date -u +%T)" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0'
bash -c 'mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] INSTRUCTIONS: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "${1:-loaded}" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0' instructions-loader

bash -c 'COUNT=${1:-0}; mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] BATCH: %s tools\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$COUNT" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0' batch-logger
